note:
企业风险投资公司康卡斯特风险投资公司(Comcast Ventures)将并入康卡斯特的业务开发部门，并将其战略转变为只专注于战略投资。
据知情人士透露，合作伙伴本周被告知了这一变化。由于讨论是私下进行的，这些知情人士要求不具名。虽然康卡斯特风险投资公司将继续在康卡斯特首席业务发展官萨姆·施瓦茨的领导下运营，但它将不再投资于一系列广泛的公司，而是只专注于能够支持康卡斯特举措的初创企业。知情人士表示，这一变化，再加上该基金独立于康卡斯特(Comcast)的独立性降低，可能会导致多名合作伙伴叛逃。
康卡斯特风险投资公司(Comcast Ventures)的一位发言人告诉CNBC，康卡斯特风险投资公司一直是一条有价值的创新渠道，提供了对邻近行业和投资机会的洞察。我们正在将我们的风险投资方法与我们的业务部门更紧密地结合起来，并将康卡斯特风险投资公司及其基金重新定位为康卡斯特有线电视的战略业务开发团队。我们整个公司的业务开发团队将继续投资于新技术和新业务，我们相信这将为康卡斯特和我们投资的公司带来更多战略机会和利益。我们将继续通过投资和战略合作伙伴关系支持我们现有的投资组合公司。
康卡斯特风险投资公司(Comcast Ventures)的基金主管艾米·班斯(Amy Banse)在9月份宣布退休。在康卡斯特风险投资公司(Comcast Ventures)工作了15年的戴夫·齐尔伯曼(Dave Zilberman)本周表示，他将加盟Norwest Venture Partners。
当班斯宣布离职时，康卡斯特表示，首席财务官迈克尔·卡瓦纳(Michael Cavanagh)将接管康卡斯特风险投资公司(Comcast Ventures)的领导权。其中一位知情人士表示，在过去两个月里，卡瓦纳和康卡斯特前首席财务官迈克尔·安杰拉基斯(Michael Angelakis)评估了该基金的战略，认为它更适合施瓦茨的业务发展部门。安杰拉基斯现在用康卡斯特提供的资金管理着私人投资公司Atairos。
施瓦茨是20多年前康卡斯特风险投资公司(Comcast Ventures)的创始合伙人之一。他将决定康卡斯特是收购公司，还是进行种子、风险投资或后期投资，哪个更好。康卡斯特将继续支持现有的康卡斯特风险投资组合。
知情人士表示，康卡斯特风险投资公司(Comcast Ventures)多年来表现最好的许多投资都是战略性投资，包括本月早些时候被Melody Investment Advisors收购的CTI Towers。随着软银(SoftBank)的愿景基金(Vision Fund)、通用大西洋(General Atlantic)和洞察合伙人(Insight Partners)等更多基金为初创企业筹集数百亿美元资金，争夺收购目标变得越来越困难。根据美国国家风险投资协会(National Venture Capital Association)的数据，过去两年，投资于美国公司的风险资金创下了纪录。
将康卡斯特风险投资公司(Comcast Ventures)的重点重新放在支持宽带分销和流媒体视频的公司上，可能有助于康卡斯特走在趋势的前列，同时在该公司最了解的投资上更加自律。
note_en:
Comcast Ventures, the corporate venture capital firm, is folding into  Comcast's business development division and shifting its strategy to focus only on strategic investments.
 Partners were informed of the change this week, according to people familiar with the matter, who asked not to be named because the discussions were private. While Comcast Ventures will continue to operate under Comcast chief business development officer  Sam Schwartz, it will no longer invest in a broad array of companies, instead focusing only on start-ups that can support Comcast initiatives. That change, combined with the fund's decreased independence from Comcast, will likely lead to a number of partner defections, the people said.
  "Comcast Ventures has been a valuable innovation pipeline, providing insight into adjacent industries and investment opportunities" a spokesperson told CNBC. "We are aligning our approach to venture investing more closely with our business units and repositioning Comcast Ventures and its fund under the strategic business development team at Comcast Cable. Our business development teams across the company continue to invest in new technology and businesses, which we believe will yield more strategic opportunities and benefits for Comcast and the companies in which we invest. We will continue to support our existing portfolio companies through investment and strategic partnership."
 Amy Banse, the head of funds for Comcast Ventures,  announced her retirement in September. Dave Zilberman, a 15-year Comcast Ventures veteran,  said this week he was leaving for Norwest Venture Partners.
 When Banse announced her departure, Comcast said CFO Michael Cavanagh would take over leadership of Comcast Ventures. In the past two months, Cavanagh and former Comcast CFO Michael Angelakis, who now runs the private investment firm Atairos with capital from Comcast, reviewed the fund's strategy and decided it's a better fit within Schwartz's business development unit, one of the people said.
 Schwartz was one of the founding partners of Comcast Ventures more than 20 years ago. He will decide if Comcast is better served acquiring companies or making seed, venture or later-stage investments. Comcast will continue to support the existing Comcast Ventures portfolio.
 Many of Comcast Ventures' best performing investments through the years have been strategic in nature, including CTI Towers, which Melody Investment Advisors  acquired earlier this month, said the people. It has become increasingly difficult to compete for targets as more funds, such as  SoftBank's Vision Fund, General Atlantic and Insight Partners, raise tens of billions of dollars for start-ups. The last two years have been record setting in terms of  venture dollars invested in U.S. companies, according to The National Venture Capital Association.
  Refocusing Comcast Ventures on companies that support broadband distribution and streaming video could help Comcast stay ahead of trends while being more disciplined on investments the company understands best.
title: 康卡斯特正在将康卡斯特风险投资公司(Comcast Ventures)合并为企业业务部门，并正在转变战略，只专注于战略投资
title_en: Comcast is folding Comcast Ventures into corporate business division and is shifting its strategy to focus only on strategic investments

note:
Aurora Innovation是一家初创公司，由自动驾驶汽车行业的三名资深人士创立，他们领导了谷歌、特斯拉和优步的项目。该公司正在就收购优步ATG进行谈判。交易条款仍不得而知，但消息人士称，两家公司自去年10月以来一直在进行谈判，而且谈判进展得很顺利。
优步发言人拒绝置评，称公司的总体政策是不对此类调查发表评论。Aurora的一位发言人说，该公司对猜测不予置评。
谈判可能会受挫。但如果成功，他们有可能将极光的员工人数增加两倍，并使优步能够剥离一项代价高昂的长期业务，而这项业务在其短暂的生命周期内一直饱受争议。
在剥离优步ATG之前，近几个月来发生了一系列剥离或其他交易，这些交易将优步的重点和成本缩小到叫车和送货这两个核心领域。两年前，优步的商业模式可以被描述为“所有上述方式”，押注于从所有形式的交通工具中获得收入，包括叫车、微移动、物流、包裹和食品递送，甚至有朝一日自动驾驶机器人出租车。
自从优步上市以来，这一战略发生了变化，随着新冠肺炎疫情颠覆了经济，从根本上改变了人们的生活方式，这一战略进一步加速。在过去11个月里，优步出售了共享微移动部门Jump，出售了其不断增长但仍未盈利的物流部门优步货运(Uber Freight)的部分股份，并收购了Postmate。(对PostMates的收购预计将在2020年第四季度完成)。
优步ATG一直是该公司最后一大笔昂贵的股份。Uber ATG拥有很多长期承诺和高昂的当前成本；Uber去年11月报告称，在截至2020年9月30日的9个月里，ATG和其他技术(包括Uber Elevate)净亏损3.03亿美元。优步在S-1文件中表示，其ATG和其他技术项目的研发费用为4.57亿美元。
四位业内消息人士告诉TechCrunch，优步今年一直在向包括汽车制造商在内的几家公司收购ATG。消息人士还告诉TechCrunch，Uber ATG可能面临一轮下行，这可能是与Aurora谈判背后的另一个动机。
Aurora成立于2017年，专注于打造全自动驾驶系统，这项基础技术将允许车辆在没有人工驾驶的情况下在高速公路和城市街道上导航。Aurora吸引了Greylock Partners、红杉资本(Sequoia Capital)、亚马逊(Amazon)和T.Rowe Price等知名风险投资公司的关注和投资，部分原因是它的创始人斯特林·安德森(Sterling Anderson)、德鲁·巴格内尔(Drew Bagnell)和克里斯·厄姆森(Chris Urmson)。
厄姆森曾领导过谷歌的自动驾驶项目，后来该项目剥离成了Alphabet公司的Waymo。安德森最出名的是领导特斯拉Model X和该汽车制造商的自动驾驶计划的开发和推出。巴格内尔是卡内基梅隆大学(Carnegie Mellon)的副教授，帮助启动了优步在自主方面的努力，最终领导了匹兹堡高级技术中心(Advanced Technologies Center)的自主与感知团队。
Aurora已经从一家小型初创公司成长为一家拥有600名员工的公司，业务遍及旧金山湾区、德克萨斯州匹兹堡和蒙大拿州博兹曼(Bozeman)，后者是Aurora在2019年收购的激光雷达公司Blackmore的总部所在地。根据领英(LinkedIn)的记录，奥罗拉目前约有12%的员工曾在优步(Uber)工作。
尽管有这样的增长，但与优步(Uber)持有多数股权的自动驾驶子公司优步ATG(Uber ATG)相比，极光仍然相形见绌。优步ATG拥有1200多名员工，在匹兹堡、旧金山和多伦多等多个地点开展业务。根据提交给美国证券交易委员会(Securities And Exchange Commission)的文件，优步(Uber)持有优步ATG 86.2%的股份(在完全稀释的基础上)。其投资者合计持有优步ATG 13.8%的股份。
优步向自动驾驶汽车技术的公开飞跃始于2015年初，当时该公司宣布与卡内基梅隆大学(Carnegie Mellon University)国家机器人中心(National Robotics Center)建立战略合作伙伴关系。这项致力于开发无人驾驶汽车技术的协议导致优步挖走了数十名NREC的研究人员和科学家。一年后，随着内部反病毒开发项目的启动，当时由联合创始人特拉维斯·卡兰尼克(Travis Kalanick)领导的优步收购了一家名为Otto的自动驾驶卡车初创公司。
这笔收购几乎从一开始就陷入了困境。当年早些时候，谷歌的明星工程师安东尼·莱万多夫斯基(Anthony Levandowski)和另外三名谷歌资深人士：利奥尔·罗恩(Lior Ron)、克莱尔·德劳奈(Claire Delaunay)和唐·伯内特(Don Burnette)共同创立了。不到八个月后，优步收购了奥托。
收购两个月后，谷歌对莱万多夫斯基和罗恩提出了两项仲裁要求。优步并未参与这两项仲裁。在仲裁进行的同时，Waymo在2017年2月单独对优步提起诉讼，指控其窃取商业机密和侵犯专利。Waymo在这起诉讼中声称，Levandowski窃取了商业机密，然后这些商业机密被优步使用。诉讼经过审判，但在2018年以和解告终。
根据和解协议，优步同意不将Waymo的机密信息纳入他们的硬件和软件。优步还同意支付一项财务和解协议，其中包括0.34%的优步股权，根据其G-1系列融资720亿美元的估值。当时计算的优步股本约为2.448亿美元。
在收购奥托的初期，优步估计，到2019年，它可能会有7.5万辆自动驾驶汽车上路，到2022年，它将在13个城市运营无人驾驶出租车服务，这份文件是由TechCrunch公布的，最先报道了这份文件。为了实现这些雄心勃勃的目标，这家叫车公司每月花费2000万美元开发自动驾驶技术。
优步从未接近达到这些目标，这一任务因技术障碍、与Waymo的诉讼、与莱万多夫斯基的麻烦关系，以及2018年3月该公司一辆自动驾驶测试车辆在亚利桑那州坦佩发生的致命撞车事故而脱轨。
事故发生后，优步暂停了所有测试，并在过去18个月里缓慢加大了面向公众的业务。开发自动驾驶汽车的成本高昂，促使优步在2019年春天剥离了该公司，此前该公司完成了来自丰田(Toyota)、汽车零部件制造商电装(Denso)和软银(Softbank)愿景基金(Vision Fund)的10亿美元融资。
剥离事件发生在优步上市前一个月左右，几个月来一直是外界猜测的对象。这被视为优步与其他投资者分担昂贵负担的一种方式，并使其能够专注于核心竞争力和更近期的利润目标。
撇开麻烦不谈，优步ATG有两个重要而关键的特点，使它对奥罗拉具有吸引力：人才和丰田。
在2019年注资之前，这家日本汽车巨头已经向优步投资了5亿美元。当时，两家公司宣布，他们打算在2021年将基于丰田Sienna的自动化拼车的试点规模部署到优步拼车网络中，“利用优步ATG的自动驾驶技术和丰田卫士先进的安全支持系统的优势。”
2019年对优步ATG部门的投资加深了丰田与该公司的关系。
“当优步在商业秘密诉讼中与Waymo对峙时，Aurora轰动一时。在18个月内，奥拉与现代、拜顿和大众集团达成了几种合作伙伴关系。有些失败了，但也有新的收益，特别是菲亚特克莱斯勒汽车(Fiat Chrysler Automobiles)。这种音乐椅般的变化突显了自动驾驶业务中充满希望的参与者的绝对数量--这个市场仍然充满了商业和技术上的未知数--以及现有汽车制造商在寻找最好的技术和交易方面的反复无常。
大众集团曾在2018年1月吹捧其与Aurora的合作伙伴关系，该集团在2019年6月向TechCrunch证实，“我们合作伙伴关系下的活动已经结束。”大众集团(VW Group)最终将其资本投入了另一家自动驾驶汽车技术开发商Argo AI，后者已锁定了对福特的支持，并与福特达成了一项客户协议。
尽管现代确实拥有极光的少数股权，但它也在2019年秋季与自动驾驶技术公司Aptiv成立了一家合资企业。根据与Aptiv的协议，双方在新的合资公司(现在称为Motional)中持有50%的股权。两家公司对Motional的投资总额将达到40亿美元(包括综合工程服务、研发和知识产权的价值)。
尽管如此，奥罗拉还是取得了胜利。去年春天，该公司在红杉资本(Sequoia)牵头的B轮融资中筹集了5.3亿美元，亚马逊(Amazon)和T·罗威·普莱斯(T.Rowe Price)进行了“重大投资”。奥罗拉当时的估值为25亿美元。最近，业内消息人士表示，奥罗拉的活动非常活跃，尤其是围绕该公司新任业务发展副总裁大卫·马迪(David Maday)的办公室。马迪曾领导通用汽车(General Motors)的企业发展和并购团队长达21年之久。
Aurora一直表示，其全套驾驶系统--为无人机提供大脑的软硬件组合--将与车辆无关，但它的一些早期测试和合作表明，它专注于机器人出租车应用，而不是物流。奥罗拉去年开始更公开地谈论将其技术应用于长途卡车运输，并对这一应用变得更加看好，特别是在收购Blackmore之后。
奥罗拉在2020年7月宣布，它正在向德克萨斯州扩张，并计划在达拉斯-沃斯堡地区测试商业路线，混合使用菲亚特克莱斯勒太平洋小型货车和8级卡车。一小支太平洋舰队预计将最先抵达。据该公司称，这些卡车将在今年年底前在德克萨斯州上路。
目前尚不清楚的是，收购优步ATG的交易可能是如何安排的；更重要的是，它是否会保留对该企业的任何兴趣。即使Uber ATG的估值预计将出现下降，但除非Aurora能够获得额外的外部投资，或者以一种允许Uber保留部分股权的方式安排交易，否则Aurora的估值似乎超出了范围。
后者是有先例的。今年早些时候，优步(Uber)牵头向Lime进行了一轮1.7亿美元的投资。作为这一复杂安排的一部分，优步将自行车和滑板车共享部门Jump出售给了Lime。
在过去的一年里，关于优步首席执行官Dara Khosrowshahi热衷于摆脱Uber ATG的传言不时出现。但随着新冠肺炎疫情的蔓延，Khosrowshahi和其他高管开始专注于其叫车服务的核心竞争力，并在送货方面加倍努力。除了微移动部门和优步货运(Uber Freight)的剥离，优步还在国际上剥离了一些地区业务，事实证明，这些业务成本太高，无法在与强大的本土竞争对手的竞争中增长。
据两位消息人士透露，正是在Jump交易之后，出售优步ATG的兴趣与日俱增。
一位业内投资者将其描述为优步的一个有趣的b计划，这笔交易将允许该公司将ATG从账面上剥离，同时可能受益于一点好处。
note_en:
Aurora Innovation,   the startup founded by three veterans of the autonomous vehicle industry who led programs at Google, Tesla and Uber, is in negotiations to buy Uber ATG. Terms of the deal are still unknown, but sources say the two companies have been in talks since October and it is far along in the process.
 An Uber spokesperson declined to comment, citing that the company’s general policy is not to comment on these sorts of inquiries. An Aurora spokesperson said it doesn’t comment on speculation.
 The talks could falter. But if successful, they have the potential to triple Aurora’s headcount and allow Uber to unload an expensive long-term play that has sustained several controversies in its short life.
  Shedding Uber ATG would follow a string of spin offs or other deals in recent months that has narrowed Uber’s focus and costs into core areas of ride-hailing and delivery. Two years ago, Uber’s business model could be described as an “all of the above approach,” a bet on generating revenue from all forms of transportation, including ride-hailing, micromobility, logistics, package and food delivery and someday even autonomous robotaxis.
 That strategy has changed since Uber went public and has further accelerated as the COVID-19 pandemic has upended the economy and fundamentally changed how people live. In the past 11 months, Uber has dumped shared micromobility unit Jump, sold a stake in its growing but still unprofitable logistics arm, Uber Freight and acquired Postmates. (The Postmates acquisition is expected to close in the fourth quarter of 2020).
 Uber ATG has been the company’s last big, expensive holding. Uber ATG holds a lot of long-term promise and high present-day costs; Uber reported in November that ATG and “other technologies” (which includes Uber Elevate) had a net loss of $303 million in the nine months that ended September 30, 2020. In its S-1 document, Uber said it incurred $457 million of research and development expenses for its ATG and “other Technology Programs” initiatives.
 Four sources within the industry told TechCrunch that Uber “has been shopping” ATG to several companies, including automakers this year. Sources have also told TechCrunch that Uber ATG was facing a potential down round, which might have been an additional motivator behind the talks with Aurora.
 Aurora, which was founded in 2017, is focused on building the full self-driving stack, the underlying technology that will allow vehicles to navigate highways and city streets without a human driver behind the wheel. Aurora has attracted attention and investment from high-profile venture firms, management firms and corporations such as Greylock Partners, Sequoia Capital, Amazon and T. Rowe Price, in part because of its founders Sterling Anderson, Drew Bagnell and Chris Urmson.
 Urmson led the former Google self-driving project before it spun out to become the Alphabet business Waymo. Anderson is best known for leading the development and launch of the Tesla Model X and the automaker’s Autopilot program. Bagnell, an associate professor at Carnegie Mellon, helped launch Uber’s efforts in autonomy, ultimately heading the autonomy and perception team at the Advanced Technologies Center in Pittsburgh.
 Aurora has grown from a small upstart to a company with 600 employees and operations in the San Francisco Bay Area, Pittsburgh, Texas and Bozeman, Montana, home of Blackmore, the lidar company it acquired in 2019. About 12% of Aurora’s current workforce previously worked at Uber, according to records on LinkedIn.
  Despite that growth, Aurora is still dwarfed by Uber ATG, the self-driving subsidiary that is majority owned by Uber. Uber ATG has more than 1,200 employees with operations in several locations, including Pittsburgh, San Francisco and Toronto. Uber holds an 86.2% stake (on a fully diluted basis) in Uber ATG, according to filings with the  U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. Its investors hold a combined stake of 13.8% in Uber ATG.
 Uber’s public leap into autonomous vehicle technology began in earnest in early 2015 when the company announced a strategic partnership with Carnegie Mellon University’s National Robotics Center. The agreement to work on developing driverless car technology resulted in  Uber poaching dozens of NREC researchers and scientists.  A year later, with the beginnings of an in-house AV development program, Uber, then led by co-founder Travis Kalanick, acquired a self-driving truck startup called Otto.
 The acquisition was troubled almost from the start. Otto was founded earlier that year by one of Google’s star engineers Anthony Levandowski, along with three other Google veterans: Lior Ron, Claire Delaunay and Don Burnette. Uber acquired Otto less than eight months later.
 Two months after the acquisition, Google made two arbitration demands against Levandowski and Ron. Uber wasn’t a party to either arbitration. While the arbitrations played out, Waymo separately filed a lawsuit against Uber in February 2017 for trade secret theft and patent infringement. Waymo alleged in the suit, which went to trial but ended in a settlement in 2018, that Levandowski stole trade secrets, which were then used by Uber.
 Under the settlement, Uber agreed not to incorporate Waymo’s confidential information into their hardware and software. Uber also agreed to pay a financial settlement that included 0.34% of Uber equity, per its Series G-1 round $72 billion valuation. That was calculated at the time to be about $244.8 million in Uber equity.
 In the early days of the Otto acquisition, Uber estimated it could have 75,000 autonomous vehicles on the roads by 2019 and be operating driverless taxi services in 13 cities by 2022, according to court documents unsealed and first reported on by TechCrunch. To reach those ambitious goals, the ride-hailing company was spending $20 million a month on developing self-driving technologies.
  Uber never came close to hitting those targets, a mission that was derailed by technical hurdles as well as the lawsuit with Waymo, its troubled relationship with Lewandowski and the fatal crash in March 2018 involving one of its self-driving test vehicles in Tempe, Arizona.
 Uber halted all testing following the crash and has been slowly ramping up its more public-facing operations over the past 18 months. The expensive undertaking of developing autonomous vehicles prompted Uber to spin out the company in spring 2019 after it closed $1 billion in funding from Toyota, auto parts maker Denso and Softbank’s Vision Fund.
 The spin out, which occurred about one month before Uber’s debut as a publicly traded company, had been the subject of speculation for months. It was seen as a way for Uber to share the expensive load with other investors and allow it to focus on its core competencies and nearer term profit goals.
  Troubles aside, Uber ATG has two important and critical features that make it attractive to Aurora: talent and Toyota.
 The Japanese car giant had already invested $500 million into Uber prior to the 2019 injection of cash. At the time, the two companies announced their intention to bring pilot-scale deployments of automated Toyota Sienna-based ridesharing vehicles to the Uber ridesharing network in 2021, “leveraging the strengths of Uber ATG’s self-driving technology alongside the Toyota Guardian advanced safety support system.”
 The 2019 investment into the Uber ATG unit deepened Toyota’s relationship with the company.
 “While Uber was facing off against Waymo in the trade secrets lawsuit, Aurora launched with a bang. Within 18 months, Auora had secured several kinds of partnerships with Hyundai, Byton and VW Group. Some have fizzled, while there have been new gains, notably with Fiat Chrysler Automobiles. The musical chair-like changes underscores the sheer number of hopeful players in the self-driving business — a market that is still full of commercial and technical unknowns — and the fickleness of incumbent car makers in search of the best tech and deal.”
 VW Group, which had touted its Aurora partnership in January 2018,  confirmed to TechCrunch in June 2019 that “activities under our partnership have been concluded.” VW Group ultimately put its capital behind Argo AI, another autonomous vehicle technology developer that had locked up backing and a customer deal with Ford.
 While Hyundai does have a minority stake in Aurora, it also went ahead and locked in a joint venture in fall 2019 with autonomous driving technology company Aptiv. Under the deal with Aptiv, both parties took a 50% ownership stake in the new joint company that is now called Motional. The combined investment in Motional from both companies will total $4 billion in aggregate value (including the value of combined engineering services, R&D and IP).
 Still, Aurora has had its wins. The company raised $530 million last spring in a Series B round led by Sequoia with “significant investment” from Amazon and T. Rowe Price. Aurora’s post-money valuation at the time was $2.5 billion. More recently, sources  in the industry say that Aurora is abuzz with activity, particularly around the office of David Maday, the company’s new vice president of business development who led General Motors’ corporate development and mergers and acquisitions team for 21 years.
 Aurora has always stated that its full driving stack — the combined suite of software and hardware that provides the brains for an AV — would be vehicle agnostic, but some of its early testing and partnerships suggested it was focused on robotaxi applications, not logistics. Aurora started talking more openly last year about applying its technology to long-haul trucking and has become more bullish on that application, particularly following its Blackmore acquisition.
 Aurora announced in July 2020 that it was expanding into Texas and planned to test commercial routes in the Dallas-Fort Worth Area with a mix of Fiat Chrysler Pacifica minivans and Class 8 trucks. A small fleet of Pacificas were expected to arrive first. The trucks will be on the road in Texas by the end of the year, according to the company.
  What’s unclear is how an acquisition of Uber ATG might be structured; and more importantly, if it will retain any interest in the enterprise. Even with the expected depletion in Uber ATG’s valuation, it would be seemingly out-of-range for Aurora unless it was able to secure additional outside investment or structure the deal in a way that would allow Uber to keep some equity.
 There is precedent for the latter. Earlier this year, Uber led a $170 million investment round into Lime. As part of the complex arrangement, Uber offloaded Jump, the bike and scooter-sharing unit, to Lime.
 Rumors that Uber CEO Dara Khosrowshahi was keen to get rid of Uber ATG have popped up from time to time in the past year. But as the COVID-19 pandemic took hold, Khosrowshahi and other executives began to focus on its core competency of ride-hailing and double down on delivery. In addition to its micromobility unit and the Uber Freight spinoff, it has divested itself internationally of a number of regional operations that were proving too costly to grow in competition with strong local rivals.
 It was on the heels of the Jump deal that interest in selling off Uber ATG ramped up, according to two sources.
 One investor in the industry described it as an interesting plan b for Uber, a deal that would allow the company to take ATG off the books, while potentially getting to benefit from a little of upside.
title: 消息人士：优步自10月以来一直在洽谈将旗下自动驾驶汽车部门Advanced Technologies Group出售给Aurora Innovation
title_en: Sources: Uber has been in talks since October to sell its self-driving car unit, Advanced Technologies Group, to Aurora Innovation

note:
华盛顿--联邦官员批准TikTok及其中国母公司字节跳动有限公司(ByteDance Ltd.)将完成资产剥离交易的最后期限延长15天，这是特朗普政府将这家社交媒体应用程序转变为一家美国公司的努力的又一次推迟。
TikTok周五向华盛顿特区美国地区法院提交的文件显示，美国外国投资委员会(Committee on Foreign Investment in the U.S.)批准将最后期限延长至11月27日。美国外国投资委员会是一个监督跨境并购的美国小组。
TikTok拒绝置评。这款视频分享应用对特朗普政府要求其在不出售的情况下关闭的命令的合法性提出了质疑。
美国辩称，它正在努力阻止美国TikTok用户的数据与中国的威权政府共享，TikTok表示永远不会允许这样做。
最近几天，特朗普政府迫使TikTok出售或关闭这款应用的努力越来越多地陷入了TikTok及其盟友的法律挑战之中。最初的最后期限是周四，但这场法律辩论很可能会持续数周。
美国商务部周四表示，将无法有效执行其命令，迫使TikTok关闭，等待诉讼结果。商务部令将禁止公司为该应用程序提供互联网托管或内容交付服务，这一禁令将阻碍其在美国的运营。
在宣布这一决定时，商务部援引了美国费城地区法官温迪·比特斯通(Wendy Beetlestone)上个月发布的初步禁令，三位TikTok明星、喜剧演员道格拉斯·马兰德(Douglas Marland)、时尚大师科塞特·里纳布(Cosette Rinab)和音乐家亚历克斯·钱伯斯(Alex Chambers)提起了诉讼。
TikTok还向美国华盛顿巡回上诉法院(U.S.Court of Appeals for the D.C.Circuit)提交了一份请愿书，要求宣布剥离令无效。该案的时间表显示，初步诉状将在12月的大部分时间提交，如果不是更久的话。
美国对中国社交媒体应用的打击是由特朗普总统领导的，目前尚不清楚当选总统乔·拜登(Joe Biden)将如何应对这种情况。两党的许多国会议员都对中国可能在美国进行的数据收集和监控活动敲响了警钟。
这一结果对美中关系以及全球互联网的未来形态具有重要影响，因为其他国家正在努力解决如何将中国科技公司纳入其中的问题。
note_en:
WASHINGTON—Federal officials have granted TikTok and its Chinese parent ByteDance Ltd. a 15-day extension of a deadline for completing a divestiture deal, in another delay of the Trump administration’s efforts to turn the social-media app into an American company.
  The deadline extension to Nov. 27 was granted by the Committee on Foreign Investment in the U.S., a U.S. panel that monitors cross-border mergers and acquisitions, according to a filing by TikTok in U.S. District Court in Washington, D.C., on Friday
  TikTok declined to comment. The video-sharing app has challenged the legality of a Trump administration order requiring its shutdown if it isn’t sold.
    The U.S. has argued that it is trying to prevent data on American TikTok users from being shared with China’s authoritarian government, which TikTok says it would never allow.
    The Trump administration’s efforts to force a sale or shutdown of the app has been increasingly bogged down in legal challenges by TikTok and its allies in recent days. The original deadline was Thursday, but it is likely that the legal debate will stretch on for weeks.
  The Commerce Department said Thursday it  wouldn’t be able to enforce its order effectively forcing TikTok to shut down pending the outcome of litigation. The Commerce order would have barred companies from providing internet-hosting or content-delivery services for the app, a prohibition that would hamstring its operations in the U.S.
  In announcing its decision, the Commerce Department cited a preliminary injunction against the shutdown issued last month by U.S. District Judge Wendy Beetlestone in Philadelphia in a suit brought by three TikTok stars, comedian Douglas Marland, fashion guru Cosette Rinab and musician Alex Chambers.
    TikTok  also has filed a petition in the U.S. Court of Appeals for the D.C. Circuit, asking for the divestiture order to be invalidated. A schedule in that case shows that initial pleadings are set to be filed through much of December, if not beyond.
  The U.S. crackdown on Chinese social-media apps has been led by President Trump, and it is unclear how President-elect Joe Biden will address the situation. Many members of Congress in both parties have sounded alarms about potential Chinese data-gathering and surveillance in the U.S.
  The outcome has important implications for U.S.-Chinese relations, as well as the future shape of the global internet, as other countries wrestle with how to incorporate Chinese technology companies.
title: 申请：CFIUS将ByteDance剥离美国TikTok业务的最后期限延长15天至11月27日
title_en: Filing: CFIUS extends the deadline for ByteDance to divest its US TikTok operations by 15 days until Nov. 27

note:
最新的进展之一是，Udemy在F轮融资中筹集了至多1亿美元，这轮融资对该公司的估值最高可达33.2亿美元。Udemy提供的市场目前有约13万门基于视频的课程，涉及65种语言，从学习Python或如何更好地拍照，到掌握正念和商业分析。
该公司已经在特拉华州提交了筹款文件，最初是由贾斯汀·拜尔斯(Justin Byers)和Prime独角兽指数(Prime Unicorn Index)的团队发现的。目前还不清楚这轮融资是否已经结束，以及是否筹集了全部资金(或者确实更多)。
记者联系乌德米寻求回应，他没有否认这一报道，但也拒绝暂时发表任何言论。一位发言人通过电子邮件对我说：“我们公司的政策是不对猜测发表评论。”“我们目前无可奉告，但如果有任何变化，我会联系你的。”
此次融资对Udemy来说将是一个强有力的举措，该公司今年早些时候才结束了E系列融资-这轮融资将该公司的估值推高至20亿美元以上。
但那是在2月份，在这种新型冠状病毒真正席卷世界之前。自那以后，从今年春季开始，专注于教育的初创企业的业务激增，因此，看到了支持后起之秀的好时机的投资者的关注也随之激增。
看看最近的一些交易，上周，Udacity宣布了一轮7500万美元的债务融资，并表示终于实现了盈利。去年10月，Kahoot宣布从软银(SoftBank)获得2.15亿美元的融资。今年9月，OutSchool筹集了4500万美元(现在已经盈利)；荷马筹集了5000万美元(来自一群令人印象深刻的战略支持者)；Unacademy筹集了1.5亿美元；巨头Byju‘s从银湖获得了5亿美元。
这些只是较大规模的交易中的一部分；除此之外，还有许多规模较小的融资、新的教育技术初创公司的推出以及其他势头的迹象。(顺便说一句，Prime Unicorn还指出，Duolingo也在筹集资金，如果所有股票都发行的话，估值高达22.1亿美元，最高可达3500万美元。我们仍在挖掘这一线索。)。
Udemy上一次融资是在今年早些时候，当时业务部的总裁告诉我，已经有5000万名学生按单点选购课程，而企业客户--包括阿迪达斯(Adidas)、通用磨坊(General Mills)、丰田(Toyota)、维普罗(Wipro)、Pinterest和Lyft--使用的是订阅模式。
根据其网站上的数据，它的商业用户看起来已经增长了，现在有超过7000人，到目前为止，课程注册总人数达到4亿人。这可能表明，Udemy现在正在用更多的资本探索机会。
但需要明确的是，申请文件没有详细说明谁参与了最新一轮融资，也没有详细说明融资的目的是什么。
正如我们在2月份融资时所写的那样，这笔资金来自一个单一的战略投资者，即与日本Udemy合作的日本教育出版商Benesse Holdings。Benesse更大的业务包括为儿童开发教育内容，为成人开发在线和面对面的课程，以及为其拥有的其他教育品牌(如Berlitz)开发教育内容，Udemy帮助Benesse为这些努力开发内容。
该公司的其他投资者包括Stripe、Naspers(现为Prosus)、Learn Capital、Insight Partners和Norwest Venture Partners等。
Prime独角兽指数(Prime Unicorn Index)指出，围绕最新的F系列的条款包括“与所有其他优先股和传统可转换股享有同等的清算优先权，这意味着如果有剩余收益，它们将不会参与普通股。”它还指出，Udemy的最新每股价格为24.13美元，高于E系列股票的定价为15.57美元。
note_en:
In one of the latest developments,  Udemy — which provides a marketplace currently numbering some 130,000 video-based courses across 65 languages, ranging from learning Python or how to photograph better, through to mastering mindfulness and business analytics — is raising up to $100 million in a Series F round of funding that would value the company at up to $3.32 billion.
 The company has filed paperwork for the fundraise in Delaware, first discovered by Justin Byers and the team at  Prime Unicorn Index. It’s not clear if the round has closed, and whether the full amount was raised (or indeed, more).
  Contacted for a response,  Udemy   didn’t deny the report but also declined to say anything for the moment. “We have a company policy where we don’t comment on speculations,” a spokesperson said to me via email. “We don’t have a comment at this time but I’ll reach out if anything changes.”
 The fundraise would be a strong move for Udemy, which only closed its Series E earlier this year — a  $50 million round that catapulted the company to a $2 billion+ post-money valuation.
 But that was in February, before the novel coronavirus really took hold of the world. Since then, startups focused on education have been seeing a surge of business starting in the spring of this year, and as a result, also a surge of attention from investors who see a good moment to back rising stars.
 Just looking at some of the most recent deals, last week,  Udacity announced a $75 million debt round and said it was finally profitable. In October,  Kahoot announced a $215 million round from SoftBank. And in September, Outschool  raised $45 million (and is now profitable); Homer  raised $50 million (from an impressive group of strategic backers); Unacademy  raised $150 million and the juggernaut that is Byju’s picked up  $500 million from Silver Lake.
  And these are just some of the bigger deals; there have been many  smaller fundraises, new  edtech startup launches and other  signs of momentum alongside  this. (And Prime Unicorn, incidentally, also noted that  Duolingo is also raising money, up to $35 million at a valuation of $2.21 billion if all shares are issued. We’re still digging on that lead.)
 When Udemy last raised money, earlier this year, the president of the business division told me it had clocked up 50 million students that purchase courses in an à la carte format, while enterprise customers — which include Adidas, General Mills, Toyota, Wipro, Pinterest and Lyft in a list of some 5,000 in all — use a subscription model.
 It looks like its business users have grown and now number over 7,000, according to  figures on its site, with total course enrollments now totalling 400 million to date. That could point to the opportunity that Udemy is now exploring with more capital.
 But to be clear, the filing does not detail who is in this latest round, nor what the purpose of the fundraising is.
 As we wrote at the time of the round in February, that fundraise came from a single, strategic investor, the Japanese educational publisher Benesse Holdings, which partners with Udemy in Japan. Benesse’s bigger business includes developing educational content for children and courses for adults, both online and in-person, and for other educational brands that it owns, such as  Berlitz, and Udemy helps Benesse develop content for those various efforts.
  Other investors in the company include Stripes, Naspers (now Prosus), Learn Capital, Insight Partners and Norwest Venture Partners, among others.
 Prime Unicorn Index notes that the terms surrounding this latest Series F include a “pari passu liquidation preference with all other preferred, and conventional convertible, meaning they will not participate with common stock if there are remaining proceeds.” It also noted that Udemy’s most recent price per share is $24.13, an up round from the Series E, which priced shares at $15.57.
title: 申请：在线学习市场Udemy正在筹集最多1亿美元的F系列资金，估值为33.2亿美元；Udemy在2月份筹集了5000万美元的E系列资金，估值超过20亿美元。
title_en: Filing: Online learning marketplace Udemy is raising up to $100M Series F at a $3.32B valuation; Udemy raised $50M Series E in February at a $2B+ valuation

note:
保险软件提供商Vertafort本周披露了一起数据泄露事件，承认有第三方获取了2770万名德克萨斯州司机的详细信息。
这起事件据信发生在3月11日至8月1日之间，是人为错误导致的，当时三个数据文件被无意中存储在不安全的外部存储服务中。
Vertafort表示，这些文件是从外部存储系统中删除的，但经过调查后，他们发现这些文件是在未经授权的情况下被访问的。
据该软件提供商称，这三个文件包含了2019年2月之前发放的驾照信息，该公司在其保险评级软件解决方案中使用了这些驾照。
曝光的数据包括德克萨斯州的驾照号码、姓名、出生日期、地址和车辆登记历史。
Vertafort在本周发布在其网站上的一份公开数据泄露通知中表示，社保号码或金融账户信息不包括在内。
Vertafort表示，它已将安全漏洞通知了相关当局，包括德克萨斯州总检察长、德克萨斯州公共安全部、德克萨斯州机动车管理局和联邦执法部门。
调查正在进行中。该公司现在还在通知德克萨斯州的司机，他们的数据在这次入侵中被泄露了。
Vertafort表示，它曾与一家情报公司合作，但没有发现任何证据表明泄露的数据被滥用或误用。
Vertafort说，尽管该公司没有发现任何证据，但出于对所有得克萨斯州驾照获得者的考虑，出于充分的谨慎，Vertafort向他们提供了一年的免费信用监控和身份恢复服务，以承认这些服务在此次活动以外的其他情况下提供了有价值的保护。
尽管如此，德克萨斯州的司机并不是这些天来唯一处理个人信息泄露的数据泄露事件的人。今年9月，数以千计的新南威尔士州驾照持有者的信息也在澳大利亚曝光，此前超过10万张图片同样被留在了一个不安全的亚马逊网络服务(AWS)云存储文件夹中。
note_en:
Vertafore, a provider of insurance software, has disclosed this week a data breach, admitting that a third-party accessed the details of 27.7 million Texas drivers.
     The incident is believed to have taken place sometime between March 11 and August 1, and happened as a result of human error when three data files were inadvertently stored in an unsecured external storage service.
 Vertafore said the files were removed from the external storage system, but after an investigation, they discovered that the files had been accessed without authorization.
 According to the software provider, the three files contained information on driver's licenses issued before February 2019, which the company was using for its insurance rating software solution.
 Exposed data included Texas driver license numbers, names, dates of birth, addresses, and vehicle registration histories.
 Social Security numbers or financial account information was not included, Vertafore said in a  data breach notification published on its website this week.
 Vertafore said it notified relevant authorities about the security breach, including the Texas Attorney General, the Texas Department of Public Safety, the Texas Department of Motor Vehicles, and federal law enforcement.
   An investigation is underway. The company is now also notifying Texas drivers whose data was exposed in the breach.
 Vertafore said it has worked with an intelligence firm but has not found any evidence that the leaked data has been abused or misused.
 "Although that firm did not find any evidence, to be considerate of all Texas driver license recipients and out of an abundance of caution, Vertafore is offering them one year of free credit monitoring and identity restoration services in recognition that these services offer valuable protection in other contexts beyond this event," Vertafore said.
 Still, Texas drivers aren't the only ones dealing with a data breach that exposed their personal information these days. The information of  thousands of New South Wales driver's license holders was also exposed in Australia in September after more than 100,000 images were similarly left in an unsecured Amazon Web Services (AWS) cloud storage folder.
title: 保险软件提供商Vertafort披露，在一项不安全的外部存储服务上的2770万名德克萨斯州司机的数据被外部方访问后，数据被泄露
title_en: Insurance software provider Vertafore discloses breach after data of 27.7M Texas drivers on an unsecured external storage service was accessed by external party

note:
领英(LinkedIn)周四表示，它发现了两个测量错误，导致40多万广告商在这个专业社交平台上为广告支付了过高的费用。
微软(Microsoft Corp.)旗下的这家公司在一篇博客文章中说，其工程团队在其广告产品中发现并修复了两个测量问题，这两个问题导致赞助内容活动中视频浏览量和广告印象的过度报道。该公司表示，在两年多的时间里，这些漏洞影响了41.8万多个广告商。
LinkedIn发现，通过视频广告，一些有机视频和视频广告可以在苹果公司(Apple Inc.)iOS设备上的屏幕外播放。
例如，如果LinkedIn用户在视频缓冲期间滚动经过视频广告，即使在看不到的情况下，该广告也会自动播放，但仍会被跟踪并记录为视频观看或完成。
LinkedIn的一位发言人表示，这可能导致视频点击量和点击率等措施被夸大，并向按点击量付费的广告商收取过高费用。
这位发言人说，该公司还表示，它可能在LinkedIn订阅源中夸大了赞助内容宣传活动的印象--例如，当用户旋转手机或快速移动到应用程序的其他部分时。
总体而言，该公司表示，超过90%的受影响广告商多付了不到25美元，并补充说，它将为他们未来的广告活动提供积分。
多年来，广告商一直要求提高社交媒体巨头提供的指标的透明度，并对其进行第三方审计，LinkedIn出现了这一错误。2016年，在广告商了解到Facebook两年来高估了该平台上视频广告的平均观看时间后，Facebook遭到了抨击。Facebook只统计了持续时间至少3秒的视频点击量，忽略了持续时间较短的视频点击量，因此人为地推高了点击量的平均时长。在某些情况下，早期的统计方法可能将观看视频的平均时间高估了60%到90%。
Facebook最终解决了一群小广告商就错误计算的视频指标提起的诉讼。
LinkedIn表示，该公司已于6月份开始与该行业的衡量监督机构--媒体评级委员会(Media Rating Council)就其衡量指标的审计进行谈判。LinkedIn的一位发言人表示：“我们正在与他们合作，对我们的指标进行审计。”
该公司还与甲骨文(Oracle)旗下的测量公司Moat合作，测量所谓的可看性-视频在其平台上显示的部分和持续时间。
note_en:
LinkedIn said Thursday it discovered a pair of measurement errors that led to more than 400,000 advertisers overpaying for campaigns on the professional networking platform.
  In a blog post, the Microsoft Corp.-owned company said its engineering team found and fixed two measurement issues in its ad products, which led to overreporting of video views and ad impressions on sponsored-content campaigns. The bugs affected more than 418,000 advertisers over the course of more than two years, it said.
  With video ads, LinkedIn discovered that some organic videos and video ads would play while they were off-screen on Apple Inc.’s iOS devices.
  If a LinkedIn user scrolled past a video ad while the video was buffering, for example, the ad would autoplay even when out of view, but still be tracked and logged as a video view or completion.
    That may have resulted in overstated measures including video views and view-through rates, as well as overcharging advertisers paying by the view, according to a LinkedIn spokesman.
    The company also said it may have been overreporting impressions on sponsored-content campaigns in the LinkedIn feed—for example, in cases when users would rotate their phones or quickly move to other parts of the app, the spokesman said.
    Overall, the company said more than 90% of the advertisers affected overpaid by less than $25, adding that it would provide them with credits for future ad campaigns.
    LinkedIn’s error comes after years of advertisers demanding more transparency and third-party auditing of the metrics provided by social-media giants. In 2016,  Facebook  came under fire after advertisers learned that it had  overestimated average viewing time for video ads on the platform for two years. Facebook had only been counting video views that lasted for at least three seconds, ignoring those of shorter duration and therefore artificially pushing the average length of a view higher. In some cases, the earlier counting method likely overestimated average time spent watching videos by between 60% and 90%.
  Facebook eventually  settled a lawsuit from a group of small advertisers over the miscalculated video metrics.
  LinkedIn said it began talks in June with the Media Rating Council, the industry’s measurement watchdog, on an audit of its metrics. “We are working with them to proceed with an audit of our metrics,” a LinkedIn spokesman said.
  The company is also working with  Oracle  -owned measurement firm Moat to measure so-called viewability—the portion of the video that appears on screen and for how long—on its platforms.
title: LinkedIn表示，它发现了两个视频和广告指标测量错误，导致41.8万广告商在两年多的时间里为广告支付了过高的费用
title_en: LinkedIn says it discovered a pair of video and ad metrics measurement errors that led to 418K advertisers overpaying for campaigns for over two years

五年前，几乎每一家科技公司都投资了虚拟现实，并将其视为未来。谷歌的一项努力是利用VR作为让学生进行虚拟实地考察的一种手段。随着谷歌艺术与文化(Google Arts&amp；Culture)应用程序明年关闭，探险将被并入该应用程序。
有了这款产品，教育工作者带着学生进行新的冒险，体验遥远的地方，回到过去，或者了解与他们自己不同的文化。看到教育者和学生都将我们的VR之旅融入到他们富有想象力的课程中，这真的是一件神奇的事情。
谷歌今天宣布，它将“不再支持Expeditions应用。”此外，它将在2021年6月30日之后从Google Play和App Store中删除。
探险先锋计划于2015年启动，为教育工作者提供有限的访问体验。一年后，谷歌让所有人都可以在Android上使用这款应用，但继续销售包括平板电脑、手机、虚拟现实查看器和路由器在内的专用探险套件。
现有的探险旅游不会消失，其中大部分将被转移到谷歌艺术与文化(Google Arts&amp；Culture)，后者已经是博物馆藏品和其他AR体验的大本营。它将在Android和iOS上以360版本提供，而网络将提供更广泛的访问。
在关闭这一专属体验的过程中，谷歌确实承认，在大流行期间探索世界的能力比以往任何时候都更加重要。然而，它“听说并认识到，并不是所有的学习者都能获得VR头戴式耳机的身临其境的体验。”
我们希望谷歌艺术与文化的这一产品演变将为教育工作者提供一座桥梁，让他们能够继续使用身临其境的内容来改变他们的课堂，并丰富他们的学生的学习体验。
这延续了谷歌VR努力的总体趋势，Cardboard在去年结束了对Google VR SDK的开源和积极开发。
你正在阅读朝九晚五的谷歌新闻，这些专家日复一日地发布关于谷歌及其周边生态系统的新闻。请务必查看我们的主页以获取所有最新消息，并关注Twitter、Facebook和LinkedIn上的朝九晚五谷歌(Google)，随时了解最新消息。不知从何说起？查看我们的独家报道、评论、操作指南，并订阅我们的YouTube频道
note_en:
Almost every tech company was invested in virtual reality five years ago and saw it as the future. One Google effort was to use VR as a means to let students go on virtual field trips. Expeditions is now being folded into the Google Arts & Culture app as the dedicated experience shuts down next year.
  With this product, educators took students on new adventures to experience far-away places, travel back in time or learn about cultures unlike their own. It has been truly magical to see how educators and students alike incorporated our VR tours into their imaginative curriculums.
 Google announced today it will “no longer support the Expeditions app.” Additionally, it will be removed from  Google Play and the  App Store after June 30, 2021.
 The Expeditions  Pioneer Program launched in 2015 as a limited access experience for educators. A  year later Google made the app available for all on Android, but continued to sell dedicated Expedition kits that included a tablet, phones, virtual reality viewers, and router.
  Existing Expedition tours are not going away and most of them will be moved into Google Arts & Culture, which is already home to museum collections and  other AR experiences. It will be available in 360 on Android and iOS, while the web will provide wider access.
 In shutting down the dedicated experience, Google does acknowledge how the ability to explore the world is more important than ever amid a pandemic. However, it “heard and recognize[d] that immersive experiences with VR headsets are not always accessible to all learners.”
 We hope this product evolution to Google Arts & Culture will provide educators with a bridge to continue to use immersive content to transform their classrooms and enrich the learning experience for their students.
  This continues the general wind down of Google’s VR efforts, with Cardboard open-sourced and active development on the Google VR SDK ending  last year.
  You’re reading 9to5Google — experts who break news about Google and its surrounding ecosystem, day after day. Be sure to check out  our homepage for all the latest news, and follow 9to5Google on  Twitter,  Facebook, and  LinkedIn to stay in the loop. Don’t know where to start? Check out our  exclusive stories,  reviews,  how-tos, and  subscribe to our YouTube channel

title_en: Google says it will end support for Expeditions, its education-focused VR app, on June 30, 2021, and fold most of the existing tours into its Arts & Culture app

联昌国际证券(TFI Securities)分析师郭明志(音译)表示，苹果打算在明年上半年推出一款配备迷你LED显示屏的iPad和带有类似AirPods Pro设计签名的AirPods 3。
在MacRumors看到的一份致投资者的研究报告中，郭台铭的最新预测涉及中国制造商Career Technology的命运，得益于其灵活的软板技术，预计Career Technology将在2021年成为苹果产品供应链的关键合作伙伴。
据说LCP(液晶聚合物)软板提供了一种灵活的方式将组件连接在一起，同时促进高速、低延迟的数据传输。根据这份报告，Career预计将重新获得软板上声学和光学组件的订单，该软板将用于迷你发光二极管‌iPad‌，这款产品将于2021年上半年批量生产。
郭德纲在大约八个月前表示，苹果计划在2021年底之前推出六款迷你LED产品。这些产品包括12.9英寸的‌iPad Pro‌、27英寸的iMac Pro、14.1英寸的MacBook Pro、16英寸的‌MacBook Pro‌、10.2英寸的‌‌iPad‌‌和7.9英寸的‌‌‌iPad‌‌‌Mini。
然而，郭德纲早就预测，将于2021年第四季度发布的12.9英寸‌‌iPad Pro‌‌很可能是苹果的第一款迷你LED产品，因此这一最新进展似乎表明，这位分析师的预期已经改变。
正如我们在迷你LED技术指南中所概述的那样，这种显示器大约使用1000到10000个单独的LED，相比传统的LED背光显示器有许多改进，接近OLED显示屏的性能，而没有该技术的一些缺点。
关于‌AirPods‌，这份报告重申了郭德纲的信念，即苹果正在研发第三代AirPods，将于2021年上半年投入批量生产。
新的‌‌AirPods‌‌将采用用于‌AirPods Pro‌的系统级封装，取代第二代‌‌AirPods‌‌的刚性-挠性印刷电路板+贴片技术设计，并允许采用类似于‌AirPods Pro‌的外形因素，具有更短的杆部和可更换的耳尖。
‌AirPods‌3预计将更加实惠，而且将缺乏有源噪音消除等高端功能。不过，电池续航时间可能会提高，苹果正在研发一款新的无线芯片。
郭说，Career将获得‌AirPods‌3的零部件订单，预计从2020年第二季度开始，这将对供应商的收入和利润做出重大贡献。
郭台铭在报告中还提到，苹果打算在明年扩大软板技术的使用，预计这将节省空间，降低制造成本，事业也将从更广泛的采用中受益。

Apple intends to launch an  iPad with a mini-LED display and  AirPods 3 with a design signature similar to  AirPods Pro in the first half of next year, according to TFI Securities analyst  Ming-Chi Kuo.
 In a research note to investors, seen by  MacRumors, Kuo's latest predictions relate to the fortunes of Chinese manufacturer Career Technology, which is expected to be a key partner in the supply chain for Apple's products in 2021, thanks to its flexible soft board technology.
 LCP (liquid crystal polymer) soft boards are said to offer a flexible way to connect components together while facilitating high-speed, low-latency data transfer. Career is expected to regain orders for acoustic and optical components in soft boards destined for a mini-LED ‌iPad‌, which will be mass produced in the first half of 2021, according to the note.
 Kuo said roughly eight months ago that Apple has  six mini-LED products in its pipeline planned to launch though the end of 2021. The products include a 12.9-inch ‌ iPad Pro‌, a 27-inch  iMac Pro, a 14.1-inch MacBook Pro, a 16-inch ‌MacBook Pro‌, a 10.2.-inch ‌‌iPad‌‌, and a 7.9-inch ‌‌‌iPad‌‌‌ mini.
 However, Kuo has  long predicted that a 12.9-inch ‌‌iPad Pro‌‌ with a fourth quarter 2021 launch timeframe would likely be Apple's first mini-LED product, so this latest development appears to suggest that the analyst's expectations have changed.
 As we've outlined in our  guide on mini-LED technology, the displays use on the order of 1,000 to 10,000 individual LEDs that offer a number of improvements over traditional LED-backlit displays, coming close to performance of OLED displays without some of the drawbacks of that technology.
 With regards to ‌AirPods‌, the note reiterates Kuo's belief that Apple is  working on third-generation AirPods that will go into mass production during the first half of 2021.
 The new ‌‌AirPods‌‌ will adopt the System-in-Package used for the ‌AirPods Pro‌, replacing the rigid-flex PCB+SMT design of the second-generation ‌‌AirPods‌‌, and allowing for a form factor similar to the ‌AirPods Pro‌, featuring a shorter stem and replaceable ear tips.
 The ‌AirPods‌ 3 are expected to be more affordable and will lack higher-end features like Active Noise Cancellation. Battery life could be improved, though, and Apple is working on a new wireless chip.
 Kuo says Career will obtain component orders for the ‌AirPods‌ 3, which is expected to significantly contribute to the supplier's revenue and profit from the second quarter of 2020.
 Kuo's note also mentions that Apple intends to expand its use of soft board technology in next year's " iPhone 13," which is expected to save space and reduce manufacturing costs, with Career again projected to benefit from the wider adoption.
 So not interested in mini-LED anything. I'd rather live with poor black levels than the distracting light-bleeds that multi-LED approaches bring. I'll wait for OLED or micro-LED.
  What's the light-bleed issue with mini-LED?
  There better be a "new" 11 IPP. :mad: If this thing gets killed to promote new Air4 even more, i'll be pissed.

 A new iPad Mini with slimmer bezels is top of my list. Give me one of those and I'll switch out my iPhone 12 Pro Max for an iPhone Mini, and be happy for the rest of my life.
